Vancouver Sun Run: Sign up & event info

High-profile Hells Angel shot outside his Burnaby home   Ronaldo Lising, the first of the biker gang to be criminally convicted in B.C., is expected to survive his injuries after the targeted shooting on Monday morning.
Man shot dead in Surrey hotel parking lot Monday in broad daylight
Black bear seen stalking gosling in Burnaby's Central Park is destroyed
COVID-19: Third Omicron wave has begun in B.C. and it's expected to grow rapidly
Negotiations collapse between provincial government and BCGEU workers
Student visiting from Quebec dies after B.C. highway crash   The crash between a B.C. Transit bus and a Purolator cube van happened at about 4:30 p.m. on June 30
Teenager seriously injured in stabbing at Surrey high school   Two youth have been arrested in connection with the stabbing at Panorama Ridge Secondary.

Vaughn Palmer: NDP leadership race provides chance to raise old growth, fracking and LNG issues   Opinion: John Horgan’s pending retirement provides opening to challenge his trade-offs on environmental issues.
Where's the $500 the federal government promised B.C. renters?   Rental advocacy groups and provincial government have not yet heard when or how the cash will be distributed

Vancouver proposes huge housing development at north end of Granville Bridge   Public responses so far runs against the development by those worried about density without amenities and an urban planning professor says density can be achieved without building towers that dominate areas of Vancouver
High-profile Hells Angel shot outside his Burnaby home
Man shot dead in Surrey hotel parking lot Monday in broad daylight
Black bear seen stalking gosling in Burnaby's Central Park is destroyed
Student visiting from Quebec dies after B.C. highway crash
COVID-19: Third Omicron wave has begun in B.C. and it's expected to grow rapidly

Hunter stalked in execution-style killing in South Africa   Riaan Naude, who often uploaded pictures of himself on social media with the dead bodies of lions, giraffes and elephants, was found dead near his vehicle in Limpopo

'Two doses are no longer enough': Canadians should expect to get COVID shot every nine months, says health minister   Federal health Minister Jean-Yves Duclos said the previous definitions of “fully vaccinated” makes no sense, explaining that it’s more important that shots are “up to date.”
FIRST READING: The new regulations that will make Canadian gasoline (even) more expensive   A B.C. MP closes her constituency offices in 'solidarity' with Roe v. Wade decision
Canadian movie depicting Hindu goddess smoking sparks outrage in India   'Hurting Hindu sentiments cannot be allowed to go unpunished,' warned one social-media commenter
World's richest billionaires lose $1.4 trillion in worst half ever   Elon Musk’s fortune plunged almost US$62 billion, while Jeff Bezos saw his wealth tumble by about US$63 billion
Port of Vancouver truckers delay job action for one month for negotiations   Members of the United Truckers Association voted unanimously on Friday in favour of labour action.

Dan Fumano: How much housing does Vancouver need? What kind? And where?   Opinion: With housing set to be a top issue, again, in this year's election, residents will use their votes to show what kind of approach they want to see.
Fortis B.C., Suncor to partner on Port Moody hydrogen pilot project   The project will be located at Suncor's Burrard Terminal site and will produce hydrogen from natural gas.

Vancouver charges some of the highest development fees among major cities: Study   A CMHC report found that the amount and number of fees can have a big influence on the cost and timeline to deliver new housing.
Soaring inflation expectations raise odds of super-sized Bank of Canada hike   Bank's surveys of businesses and consumers show growing doubt about its ability to put a lid on inflation

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Secrecy in the courts: Exclusive study reveals increasing use of publication bans in Canada   An investigation of discretionary publication bans requested during the past two years across four provinces shows a 25 per cent increase from one year to the next

Letters to The Sun, July 5, 2022: The affordability puzzle   Much has been written about housing in Vancouver, and especially about the problem of affordability. To its credit, The Sun sponsored a panel with some of the key players in Vancouver housing, including Housing Minister David Eby from government and Bob Rennie from the development industry.

John Ivison: The government-killing malaise of stagflation looms for the Liberals   Opinion: The Trudeau government has survived scandals and missteps, but nothing has sapped its support quite like high inflation

Dan Fumano: Vancouver council eyes 'non-compliant' housing proposals   Opinion: Considering Vancouver's housing crisis, should city hall consider proposals that are offside with existing rules and policies, against the advice of planners? Council weighs that question this week.

Vaughn Palmer: Bob Williams was his own man, at a time in politics when it wasn't always easy to do so   Former NDP cabinet minister Bob Williams was one of the most polarizing figures in B.C. politics from the 1960s to the 1990s.

Softwood lumber dispute is bad for consumers and producers   Opinion: This has notably led to the imposition of duties on Canadian softwood lumber, which have hurt the industry in regions like Vancouver Island

NHL Draft: Canucks writing better chapters in book that boasts beauties, busts   The Canucks have also had great draft picks since 2000 and putting them in order promotes debates. Time is the greatest measurement of true worth and so is potential.

Canucks Under the Microscope: Spencer Martin   Thatcher Demko is going to get his starts, but not the career-high 64 last season that invited fatigue and injury, and that’s where Spencer Martin has to be that reliable stopper who can do much more that play in back-to-back scenarios.
Abbotsford Canucks need a new goalie coach   Postmedia has confirmed that is likely Curtis Sanford is leaving the organization.

NHL draft prospect understands pressure-packed situations in the crease, off the ice   Top-ranked goaltender Tyler Brennan's understanding of stressful moments goes beyond battling through screens or facing shooters one-on-one.
Who will the Canucks draft? Swedish winger Liam Ohgren has big power-forward potential   A look at the players who should pique the Canucks’ interest in the upcoming NHL draft.
Things to do around Metro Vancouver July 7-13   This week’s events around Metro Vancouver include Fleet Foxes, Khatsahlano Street Party, and VCBW Craft Beer and Music Festival.
Must-see TV: Amazing Race Canada, Netflix's Boo, Bitch top this week's watch list   The Amazing Race Canada is back after a two-year break.
Drake joins Backstreet Boys in Toronto for 'I Want It That Way'   The rapper hopped onstage to sing the boy band’s 1999 hit I Want it That Way.
Harry Styles devastated over Denmark shooting, cancels concert   Three people were killed and several others wounded in a shooting at Field’s mall in the capital, Danish police said.
Steven Tyler doing 'extremely well' after rehab stint   Tyler voluntarily entered a treatment facility in May after relapsing on pain pills.

Cancelled Air Canada flights strand Quebec teens at Vancouver airport   A teen scout group heading to the Yukon for a long-planned camping trip rolling ended up rolling out their sleeping bags on the floor of YVR after their Air Canada flight was cancelled
Joy Kogawa house coming up for Vancouver heritage status consideration   After years of advocacy, the historic Joy Kogawa house is close to receiving heritage designation.

B.C. 'winged hero' who rescued injured wildlife is calling it quits. The search is on for a successor   After 27 years rescuing distressed animals from all over southwestern B.C., Norm Snihur is now on a quest to find what's arguably the most elusive quarry of his career: a successor.

Loss of beloved Value Village to fire felt in East Vancouver neighbourhood   Locals said they will miss the thrift store that had been a fixture in the neighbourhood for decades. Fire officials said the cause of the fire isn't known but they can't yet rule out arson

B.C. has the most breweries in Western Canada but the fewest beer drinkers. Why?   Buying ale now costs 6.8 per cent more than it did in the 2020 fiscal year, liquor prices rose much less, by 2.4 per cent.
Sold (Bought): Lightning-fast sale for this spacious custom-built North Vancouver house   Home sold before it even had a chance to hit the market

Flexible design layouts on offer at innovative Duo Luxury Townhomes in Parksville   Including two luxurious retreats: large bedrooms that are connected to both ensuite bathooms and big walk-in closets with plenty of storage space.

The Home Front: Furniture has Earth and your health in mind   Bridgitte Alomes founded Natural Pod ten years ago intending to produce products made from sustainable (non-toxic) materials as an alternative to fast furniture.
Brian Minter: Nothing represents a hug better than a beautiful batch of cut flowers   With some strategic planning, you can enjoy a continuous supply of cut perennials from April well into fall.
Condo Smarts: Successful strata communities result from reasonable behaviour, respect   When bylaws and rules are not enforced, troublesome individuals can take advantage.
Where are the best places to eat in Kelowna?   Linda Barnard takes a culinary tour to help you decide

Checking In: EXchange Hotel   Oh, if this barrel vault lobby could talk. There’d be fables of fortunes won and lost. Stories of scams and scoundrels. Tall tales of golden opportunities and whispered secrets of can’t miss ventures.
Santa Monica will move you   Re-discover SoCal at your own pace
Montréal neighbourhood Saint-Henri a cultural delight   What’s old is new again in trendy and historic area.
Juhl: 3 road-trip games to keep the kids from driving you around the bend   These ideas will help you pass the time in the car and you don't have to buy anything to play.
Nissan recalls vehicles over sudden hood opening, roll-away risk   37,000 older SUVs in Canada may see hoods unlatch accidentally, owners of newer trucks asked to use parking brake
Subaru wants to help you discover Canada's national parks   Park Pass program continues for Wilderness buyers
Do licence plates still need to be renewed in Ontario?   You no longer need to pay, but you do need to renew to avoid hefty fines
Dangerous driving increasingly common in Ontario: CAA survey   Despite the sharp teeth of the MOMS Act, more motorists say they feel unsafe on Ontario highways this year than last
Balancing Act: Ontario driver flips Dodge Charger on Hwy 401 ramp   When asked to retrieve his backpack from the passenger’s seat, police found it filled with cannabis products
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.
Clint Eastwood handed $2.6 million after winning a second trademark infringement CBD lawsuit   Company did not have permission to use his image or likeness.
U.S. Army turns to hemp for sniper uniforms   New suits expected to help snipers “remain undetected within close proximity of the enemy forces.”

Basketball: WNBA star Griner makes freedom appeal to Biden   The WNBA superstar was held at a Moscow airport on Feb. 17 when a search of her luggage allegedly revealed multiple cannabis oil vape cartridges.
Cannabis grow worth half a million dollars discovered in century-old church   About 350 cannabis plants found in church built in 1875.

Canadian cannabis giant Tilray offers U.S. workers coverage for abortion-related costs   Updated health care benefits will cover transportation expenses for employees and their covered family members.
